title: ERC_CoG PROMENADE - WP2 - MetaImagery (Metaphor and Mental Imagery)
description: This derivative EEG dataset from the ERC PROMENADE project investigates how different types of cues (physical, mental imagery, literal, and metaphorical) influence the neural processing of target pictures in a picture-matching task. Comprising 39 participants and 238 files (14.8 GiB), the dataset includes preprocessed EEG data with high-pass (0.10 Hz) and low-pass (45 Hz) filtering, ICA-based artifact removal, and behavioral measures of task accuracy and individual differences in vocabulary and mental imagery abilities. The study examines event-related potentials as participants judge whether target pictures match preceding cue information across four experimental conditions. The study was approved by the Ethics Committee of the Department of Brain and Behavioral Sciences of the University of Pavia (protocol number 123/2023).
methods_description: EEG data were acquired using a Brain Vision ACTICHAMP system with 64 electrodes. Preprocessing included high-pass (0.10 Hz) and low-pass (45 Hz) filtering with 4th order IIR Butterworth filters, re-referencing to average mastoid activity (TP9 and TP10), and ICA-based removal of eye-related artifacts. Data were epoched from 1.87 seconds before to 3.17 seconds after target picture onset (5.04 second epochs). Participants completed a picture-matching task with four cue types (Physical, Imagery, Literal, Metaphorical) in a counterbalanced block design with inter-stimulus intervals of 700 ms (Physical, Literal, Metaphorical) or 3000 ms (Imagery).

funding_references.award_title: PROcessing MEtaphors: Neurochronometry Acquisition and Decay (PROMENADE)
